description: PublicResource is the Schema for Pangolin public resources.

apiVersion.description:
APIVersion defines the versioned schema of this representation of an object.
Servers should convert recognized schemas to the latest internal value, and
may reject unrecognized values.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#resources

kind.description:
Kind is a string value representing the REST resource this object represents.
Servers may infer this from the endpoint the client submits requests to.
Cannot be updated.
In CamelCase.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds

spec.description: PublicResourceSpec defines the desired state of a PublicResource.

auth.description: Auth configures authentication for this resource (http only).

authSecretRef.description:
AuthSecretRef is the name of a Kubernetes Secret in the same namespace containing
sensitive auth values. Well-known keys: pincode, password, basic-auth-user, basic-auth-password.

autoLoginIdp.description: AutoLoginIdp is the Pangolin IdP ID for auto-login. 0 means not set.

ssoRoles.description: SsoRoles restricts access to users that have one of these Pangolin roles.

ssoUsers.description: SsoUsers restricts access to these Pangolin user email addresses (SSO).

whitelistUsers.description: WhitelistUsers allows only these Pangolin user email addresses.

enabled.description: Enabled controls whether this resource is active.

fullDomain.description: FullDomain is the public domain to expose (required for http protocol).

headers.description: Headers are extra HTTP headers to pass to Pangolin.

items.description: PublicHeaderSpec defines a custom HTTP header.

hostHeader.description: HostHeader sets a custom Host header on the Pangolin resource.

estimatedTime.description: EstimatedTime is the estimated maintenance duration.

message.description: Message is the maintenance page message.

title.description: Title is the maintenance page title.

type.description: Type is the maintenance type: forced or automatic.

name.description: Name is the display name of the resource in Pangolin.

proxyPort.description: ProxyPort is the Pangolin-side port (required for tcp/udp protocol).

rules.description: Rules are access control rules applied to this resource (http only).

items.description: PublicRuleSpec defines an access control rule.

match.description: Match is the attribute to match against.

priority.description: Priority controls evaluation order (lower = higher priority). Defaults to 100.

value.description: Value is the match value (IP, CIDR, path pattern, or 2-letter country code).

siteRef.description: SiteRef is the name of the NewtSite that owns this resource.

ssl.description: Ssl enables SSL on the Pangolin resource (http only).

targets.description: Targets are the backend targets for this resource.

items.description: PublicTargetSpec defines a backend target for a public resource.

enabled.description: Enabled controls whether this target is active.

healthcheck.description: Healthcheck is the health check configuration for this target.

enabled.description: Enabled controls whether health checking is active.

followRedirects.description: FollowRedirects controls whether to follow HTTP redirects.

headers.description: Headers are extra HTTP headers to include in the health check request.

items.description: PublicHeaderSpec defines a custom HTTP header.

hostname.description: Hostname is the hostname to health-check.

interval.description: Interval is the seconds between health checks.

method.description: Method is the HTTP method to use.

mode.description: Mode is the health check mode.

path.description: Path is the HTTP path to request.

port.description: Port is the port to health-check.

status.description: Status is the expected HTTP status code.

timeout.description: Timeout is the health check timeout in seconds.

unhealthyInterval.description: UnhealthyInterval is the seconds between checks when unhealthy.

method.description: Method is the internal protocol to reach the backend (required for http resources).

path.description: Path is the URL path or pattern for this target.

pathMatchType.description: PathMatchType is the path matching type.

rewritePath.description: RewritePath is the path to rewrite the request to.

rewritePathType.description: RewritePathType is the rewrite match type.

tlsServerName.description:
TlsServerName overrides the SNI name for the backend TLS connection.
Defaults to FullDomain when empty.

status.description: PublicResourceStatus defines the observed state of a PublicResource.

conditions.description: Conditions holds standard Kubernetes condition objects for this resource.

items.description: Condition contains details for one aspect of the current state of this API Resource.

lastTransitionTime.description:
lastTransitionTime is the last time the condition transitioned from one status to another.
This should be when the underlying condition changed.  If that is not known, then using the time when the API field changed is acceptable.

message.description:
message is a human readable message indicating details about the transition.
This may be an empty string.

observedGeneration.description:
observedGeneration represents the .metadata.generation that the condition was set based upon.
For instance, if .metadata.generation is currently 12, but the .status.conditions[x].observedGeneration is 9, the condition is out of date
with respect to the current state of the instance.

reason.description:
reason contains a programmatic identifier indicating the reason for the condition's last transition.
Producers of specific condition types may define expected values and meanings for this field,
and whether the values are considered a guaranteed API.
The value should be a CamelCase string.
This field may not be empty.

status.description: status of the condition, one of True, False, Unknown.

type.description: type of condition in CamelCase or in foo.example.com/CamelCase.

fullDomain.description: FullDomain is the fully-qualified public domain assigned to this resource.

niceId.description: NiceID is the human-readable resource identifier from Pangolin.

observedGeneration.description: ObservedGeneration reflects the .metadata.generation most recently reconciled.

phase.description: Phase represents the current lifecycle phase of the resource.

resourceId.description: ResourceID is the numeric resource ID assigned by the Pangolin API.

ruleIds.description: RuleIDs are the numeric IDs of the access control rules registered in Pangolin.

rulesHash.description: RulesHash is a hash of spec.rules used to detect changes and trigger rule reconciliation.

targetIds.description: TargetIDs are the numeric IDs of the backend targets registered in Pangolin.

targetsHash.description: TargetsHash is a hash of spec.targets used to detect changes and trigger target reconciliation.
